questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding the recent Goods and Services Tax (GST) rate rationalization: 1. The GST rate structure has been simplified into a two-slab system of 5% and 18%, eliminating the 12% and 28% slabs. 2. A 40% Special De-Merit Rate has been introduced for 'sin' goods, replacing the compensation cess. 3. The rationalization has effectively shifted the median tax rate from 12% to 5%. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: All statements are correct. The GST structure now has two slabs (5% and 18%). The compensation cess on 'sin' goods and select luxuries is replaced by a 40% Special De-Merit Rate. Consequently, the median tax rate has moved from 12% to 5%.
questions.takeaway: The GST rate rationalization simplified the structure to 5% and 18%, replaced the compensation cess with a 40% Special De-Merit Rate, and reduced the median tax rate to 5%.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Two-slab system of 5% and 18%, removing the earlier 12% and 28% rate. A 40% Special De-Merit Rate applies to 'sin' goods and select luxuries, replacing the compensation cess levy. The median tax rate effectively moves from 12% to 5%.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: With reference to the recent relief measures under the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime, consider the following statements: 1. Individual life and health insurance policies, along with their reinsurance, are fully exempted from GST. 2. Consumer durables such as air conditioners and all television sets have been moved to the 5% tax slab to boost middle-class consumption. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: GST is exempted on all individual life and health insurance policies and their reinsurance. Statement 2 is incorrect: Cement, air conditioners, and all TV sets have dropped from the 28% to the 18% slab, not the 5% slab.
questions.takeaway: Health and life insurance policies are now exempt from GST, while major consumer durables like ACs and TVs have been moved from the 28% to the 18% slab.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: GST is exempted on all individual life and health insurance policies and their reinsurance. Cement, air conditioners, and all TV sets have dropped from the 28% to the 18% slab.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: How many of the following pairs of goods/services and their recent GST treatment are correctly matched? 1. Individual health insurance : Exempted from GST 2. Cement : Placed in the 18% slab 3. Select luxury goods : Subject to compensation cess over and above the highest slab Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: Individual health insurance is exempted. Pair 2 is correctly matched: Cement dropped to the 18% slab. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched: The compensation cess levy on select luxuries has been replaced by a 40% Special De-Merit Rate.
questions.takeaway: The compensation cess has been replaced by a 40% Special De-Merit Rate for 'sin' goods and select luxuries.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: A 40% Special De-Merit Rate applies to 'sin' goods and select luxuries, replacing the compensation cess levy. GST is exempted on all individual life and health insurance policies... Cement, air conditioners, and all TV sets have dropped from the 28% to the 18% slab.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Under the recently rationalized Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework, how does the tax treatment of 'sin' goods differ from that of consumer durables such as air conditioners and televisions?

options.text: 'Sin' goods are subject to a compensation cess, whereas consumer durables are taxed at the peak standard rate of 28%.
'Sin' goods attract a 40% Special De-Merit Rate, whereas consumer durables have been placed in the 18% standard slab.
Both categories are taxed at the 28% slab, but 'sin' goods attract an additional compensation cess.
'Sin' goods are taxed at the highest standard slab of 18%, whereas consumer durables are placed in the 5% slab.

questions.explanation: Under the rationalized GST structure, the 28% standard slab was removed. Consumer durables like air conditioners and TVs were dropped to the 18% slab. Meanwhile, 'sin' goods and select luxuries are now subject to a 40% Special De-Merit Rate, which replaces the earlier compensation cess.
questions.takeaway: The GST rationalization eliminated the 28% standard slab, moving heavy consumer durables to 18%, while introducing a 40% Special De-Merit Rate for 'sin' goods in place of the compensation cess.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: A 40% Special De-Merit Rate applies to 'sin' goods and select luxuries, replacing the compensation cess levy... Cement, air conditioners, and all TV sets have dropped from the 28% to the 18% slab.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt:
With reference to the recent Goods and Services Tax (GST) relief measures for the health and insurance sectors, consider the following statements:
1. The GST exemption provided to individual life and health insurance policies also covers their reinsurance.
2. Prior to these relief measures, the health sector items that were exempted or moved to the 5% slab were generally taxed at 18%.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct. The recent GST relief measures exempted all individual life and health insurance policies, explicitly including their reinsurance to prevent tax cascades. The text notes that these health sector items were transitioned from an 18% rate down to either Exempt status or the 5% slab.
questions.takeaway: Tax relief for the health sector under the rationalized GST regime includes a drop from the 18% rate to Exempt/5%, notably fully exempting individual life and health insurance along with their reinsurance.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Relief for Citizens and Key Sectors: Health (18% to Exempt/5%): GST is exempted on all individual life and health insurance policies and their reinsurance.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the structural adjustments in the recent Goods and Services Tax (GST) rate rationalization:
1. The elimination of the 12% and 28% tax slabs has effectively shifted the median tax rate of the GST system to 5%.
2. The rationalization ensures that no category of goods is taxed at a rate higher than the new peak standard slab of 18%.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the removal of the 12% and 28% slabs to create a two-slab system (5% and 18%) effectively moved the median tax rate from 12% to 5%. Statement 2 is incorrect; while 18% is the highest standard slab, 'sin' goods and select luxuries are taxed at a higher 40% Special De-Merit Rate.
questions.takeaway: The rationalization simplified the GST standard slabs to 5% and 18%, lowering the median rate to 5%, but maintained a higher 40% Special De-Merit Rate for 'sin' goods.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Two-slab system of 5% and 18%, removing the earlier 12% and 28% rate. A 40% Special De-Merit Rate applies to 'sin' goods and select luxuries... The median tax rate effectively moves from 12% to 5%.
